L1.area: Cryptographic arithmetic and mathematical utility extensions

L2.description: A vulnerability exists in the big integer modular exponentiation implementation where negative exponents are not properly handled. An attacker can supply a crafted negative exponent to force the computation of incorrect results, leading to signature verification bypass.

L3.area: Big integer extended arithmetic and modular exponentiation support
L3.description: A vulnerability exists in the big integer modular exponentiation implementation where negative exponents are not properly handled. When a negative exponent is passed to the modular power computation, the sign is ignored and the algorithm processes only the magnitude bits, producing mathematically incorrect results. An attacker can exploit this to force the computation of incorrect modular inverses and bypass RSA signature verification.

ground_truth.reason: The bnModPow function does not handle negative exponents, causing it to compute incorrect modular exponentiation results, which enables an attacker to bypass signature verification.
